During the Cold War, leadership was shared between the United States and the Soviet Union. In the United States, Presidents Truman, Eisenhower, Kennedy, Johnson, Nixon, Ford, Carter, Reagan, and Bush were in charge at different times. In the Soviet Union, leaders such as Stalin, Khrushchev, Brezhnev, Andropov, and Chernenko held power. Their actions shaped the tense relationship between the two superpowers throughout the period.

P.P.S.: The tension between the two powers was tightly controlled through treaties and threats. The time taken to manage this situation was tremendous. The tactics taken by both sides tested the strength of their alliances. The threat of nuclear war terrified the world. The time taken to reach détente was tremendous. The treaties took time to take effect. The tensions took time to take a toll. The test of leadership took time to take shape. The tactics took time to take hold.
